variant: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

request.message: give me the complete guide: table of products, weekly timeline, checklist, and key stats for starting skincare from zero

response_body.response:
# skincare from zero: complete starter guide you're 17, so your skin is still adapting. the core principle: **build a strong barrier first, then add actives one at a time.** most beginners tank their skin by stacking too many products too fast. ---  ---  ---  ---  --- ## key rules **one active at a time.** stacking acids, retinoids, and vitamin c overwhelms skin and kills absorption. pick azelaic if you have acne, retinoid if you want anti-aging or texture control. **barrier first.** if your skin stings, flakes, or feels tight after washing, pause actives and use ceramides + panthenol for 1–2 weeks.

a damaged barrier makes everything worse. **SPF is non-negotiable.** UV worsens acne, pigmentation, and collagen breakdown. apply every morning. **don't treat symptoms.** acne without fixing sleep/diet/hormones won't clear.

pigmentation without reducing inflammation won't fade. external products can't outrun internal chaos.

start with the 2-week foundation phase. your skin will tell you if the barrier is intact.

then add one active and watch for 4 weeks before changing anything.

products.name: The Ordinary Azelaic Acid Suspension 10%
products.description: Azelaic acid for redness, PIH, and mild rosacea — pregnancy-safe.

products.name: CeraVe PM Facial Moisturizing Lotion
products.description: Lightweight ceramide + niacinamide moisturizer for nightly use.

visual_blocks.title: starter product stack by skin type

rows: lotion on t-zone, richer on cheeks

steps.label: week 1–2: foundation
steps.detail: cleanser + moisturizer + SPF only.  no actives. let skin adjust.  watch for irritation, dryness, or reactivity.

steps.label: week 3–4: first active
steps.detail: add ONE active (azelaic if acne-prone, retinoid if anti-aging/texture). PM only, 2–3x per week.  pair with panthenol-rich moisturizer.

steps.label: week 5–6: frequency ramp
steps.detail: increase active to 4–5x per week if no irritation. monitor barrier (no stinging, flaking, or persistent redness).

steps.label: week 7–8: stabilize
steps.detail: active at maintenance frequency.  skin should feel hydrated, clear, non-reactive. ready to assess next step (second active or higher dose).

items: pick your skin type (oily/acne, dry/sensitive, or combo)
buy: gentle cleanser, hydrating moisturizer with ceramides, broad-spectrum SPF 30+
use cleanser + moisturizer + SPF for 2 weeks with zero actives
week 3: introduce ONE active (azelaic or retinoid), PM only, 2–3x per week
pair active with panthenol-rich moisturizer to support barrier repair
do NOT stack acids + retinoids + vitamin c.  one active at a time.
apply SPF every morning, no exceptions (UV worsens acne, pigmentation, and aging)
track: redness, dryness, stinging, breakouts. if barrier is damaged, pause actives and focus on ceramides + panthenol
by week 8, assess skin.  if clear and non-reactive, you can increase active frequency or add a second product

cards.label: non-negotiable daily
cards.hint: UV worsens acne, pigmentation, collagen breakdown

cards.label: target active frequency by week 6
cards.hint: if no irritation or barrier damage
